scenario_results.name: Multi-cloud telemetry architecture and evidence baseline
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves source-backed infrastructure observability recommendations with metrics, logs, traces, events, provider-native records, explicit tradeoffs, and evidence packet structure.

scenario_results.name: Prioritized signal architecture and ownership model
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves prioritized signal architecture, owner mapping, SLI linkage, record maintenance, and ownerless-record escalation.

scenario_results.name: SLO burn-rate alert policy and pager governance
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves SLO alerting, burn-rate and pager governance, alert record update notes, evidence requirements, and review cadence.

scenario_results.name: Telemetry loss and stale dashboard triage
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves operational playbook output for degraded telemetry, stale dashboards, fallback signals, evidence capture, and incident-command boundary.

scenario_results.name: Retention, cardinality, and cost tradeoff
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves retention, sampling, indexing, cardinality, cost, and residual-risk guidance while escalating legal retention and spend approval.

scenario_results.name: Multi-region and multi-tenant isolation evidence
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves isolation-safe query, workspace, metrics-scope, redaction, alert-routing, evidence-access, and privacy escalation handling.

scenario_results.name: Tool migration and dual-write verification
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves migration sequencing, dual-write verification, source-of-truth transition, acceptance criteria, rollback criteria, and non-implementation boundary.

scenario_results.name: Recovery verification after regional outage
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves recovery telemetry checks, freshness checks, SLO/error-budget review, handoff criteria, and refusal of final incident closure authority.

scenario_results.name: Record intake with missing service topology attachment
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves missing required document handling, blocked intake, retrieval dependency mapping, and no unsupported topology assumptions.

scenario_results.name: Unauthorized dashboard and alert record change
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves unauthorized or unsupported record change refusal, preservation of current records, approver identification, and audit-trail summary.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting telemetry records and source precedence
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves conflicting records escalation, source precedence questions, and refusal to finalize alert policy from contradictory records.

scenario_results.name: Industry-specific evidence gap for regulated telemetry
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves cross-industry constraints materially change the pack, with blocked retention/publication guidance when classification and authority facts are missing.

scenario_results.name: Normal in-scope execution with cloud-native terminology
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves realistic provider-native artifact handling for EKS, CloudWatch, Kubernetes events, Prometheus alerts, OpenTelemetry collector status, and incident tickets.

scenario_results.name: Boundary rejection for production Terraform and application code
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of production Terraform and application instrumentation implementation with routing to IaC and application engineering owners.

scenario_results.name: Boundary rejection for application profiling request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal for primary application performance tuning and deep code profiling outside platform observability scope.

scenario_results.name: Adjacent security, IAM, legal, and contract request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves routing for IAM, encryption, legal/compliance, pricing, and vendor-contract authority while preserving observability evidence requirements.

scenario_results.name: Low-confidence final policy request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves low-confidence escalation when RTO/RPO, SLOs, topology, tenant boundaries, incident history, and on-call ownership are missing.

scenario_results.name: Tenant-specific adaptation without generic drift
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves tenant-specific adaptation that preserves the public baseline, provider-native records, redaction, residency, approval dependencies, and rollback of tenant overrides.

notes: All 18 refreshed scenarios passed against the validated standard validation profile.

Scenario coverage includes required output fidelity, record intake and maintenance, retention and publication boundaries, unauthorized change refusal, missing attachment handling, conflicting-record escalation, industry-specific execution, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, tenant adaptation, and no unreviewed regressions.
The prior built package is superseded as baseline context by this spec-first package.
